title: 1/5/20 Google Earthing Regions 6 & 7 Ancient Rome FOR KIDS

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, for January 5th, 2020. On Sunday, it's 10.08 a.m.
So, um, I, uh, I, uh, I'm doing the video as I'm going to sleep because, um, I, uh, like, um, am remembering to do the video.
So, uh, instead of when I get up, I've been up all night long. Um, when did I, when did I start working? Like, two in the afternoon or three in the afternoon when I got up.
So, uh, yesterday, um, yeah, when I got up, I was, I started working on Region 6 because I was done with Region, I just got done with Region 3, um, at the end of the day.
And, um, and, uh, like, um, I, um, so I got started. I was going to work on, uh, Baza Diocletian, but I didn't because I looked at these other buildings in Region 6 that I didn't do.
So I started doing them, and it was really time-consuming because I had to, uh, levelify, um, uh, pillars.
Um, that's the other time-consuming thing that you have to do when you're going to build something in Google Earth.
If it's, if all the different pillars are on, uh, um, undulation. So I, uh, yeah, but then I was, uh, yeah, building, finishing different buildings.
But then I got to a point where I saw these buildings that were in Region 7, like, in the corner of it.
And, uh, I mean, Region, oh, yeah, because I started working on Region 7.
Because I saw a building that wasn't on Region 6, and I thought, well, is it on Region 7? And it's not there either.
So when I was working on it, I must have skipped that building because I didn't know which region to put in or something.
So there was one that I didn't trace. I traced all these before.
So that's when I looked at Region 7, and I said, I saw that I hadn't done it yet.
I thought it was done or something for some reason.
So I, that's when I abandoned Region 6.
Started working on Region 7 because it's smaller.
Just wanted to get out of the way.
But, huh?
It had this building on it.
The, um, forum of something, the government of something, that, uh, was really time-consuming.
I saw that there were stairs in there that I didn't build before, and they're huge.
It has, uh, it's kind of like the, um, factory of Severus that's on the Palatine Hill.
It has, has these huge flights of stairs that go up on the outside and then up to the next level on the inside.
And there were 150 stairs, um, going up to the first level.
So there's two of those.
So that's 300.
And then there was, like, and then there's the other one up that are almost as big.
So it's almost 600 that I have to do.
So anyways, yeah, that's why it's 10 in the morning, and I'm only now doing the video.
So, um, I, uh, but I'm not going to finish today.
I finished the first one with 150, and then I did the other one.
I, I, I, I, so I did 70, and then I did the other one to, like, 40 coming up from first level, second.
So, yeah, so I'll do that.
I wanted to, to trace it how he had it so that it, you can see how big it is when you can see the individual stairs.
It's worth it, I think.
And, um, cause, you know, not just from far away from ground level, cause, you know, you can do tours, video tours of ground level with what you can make with the Polygon tool.
See, that's the other cool thing about building stuff with Google Earth.
It's kind of like Minecraft.
So, um, yeah, I noticed that this building, cause it, the reason it has the stairs so high up is that it goes from the, uh, the road in the valley up to the top of the hill.
And, uh, I calculated that this building has three levels.
And it's this huge, huge building, and it's got all these different, um, pillars on it.
And so that's what I figured before.
I was looking at these buildings and I'm going, why do they have so many pillars?
And, uh, and I figured it must be because they're multi-level and they need them to support it, you know?
And, and, uh, so that explains it.
Cause I saw the stairs next to it that took it to all the different levels.
So there's this weather one that looks like that in region nine and I didn't see the stairs next to it.
So I'm going to program in, draw in my own stairs just to make it make sense to me.
I'm interested to see what other people like the actual Roman archaeological architectural historians think about it.
Think about what I created and just see how they would do it.

One interesting thing about, uh, the polygon tool on Google earth is that it's very easy to edit and any person without training can just download my file and go in there and you can edit the different files, you know, and make it change it how you want.
So I would, you know, I'm going to invite people to do that, to find the perfect model of how it was in the past or just different people's idea of it.
Cause nobody really knows, you know, exactly what it looks.
So just build something beautiful and then I guess people can decide, but, um, just by kind of like Wikipedia, just by consensus.
But, uh, um, yeah, so, uh, I was listening to quantum mechanics videos over and over again and there's some really good ones.
There's this one of this guy doing the lecture to a computer programmers is an hour and a half and it's, um, saying some cool stuff.
Somehow they managed to send a, um, a qubit up into a satellite and then do quantum entanglement experiments between one on earth and one on the satellite.
Anyway, I want to, yeah, listen to this stuff till I figure it out, but it's kind of hard to follow along when you're tired, high, and kind of even concentrating, I guess I'm feeling that.
So I'll just do it again.
When I get up, I spilled all my weed cause I was holding it in my hand cause I was tired, got clumsy or something and whacked it out of my hand and all fell out.
And it was when there was just little bits.
So I had to put it in and my lungs are really sensitive.
And each time you pick up a little bit, a little hair comes with it and those little hairs give me asthma.
So I don't know if, I don't know if it'll even be worth it to, uh, smoke this stuff.
Cause I was, I was doing fine.
I can smoke weed all day long as long as it's hermetically sealed weed, untampered weed and not get asthma at all.
I barely even use my Advair, but as soon as it's tampered, that's when I get really bad asthma.
Uh, you know, like if you, if you drop a little bud on like the ground or the floor, even on the table, no matter what it, the static electricity, like picks up dust and then your lungs detected.
And yeah, it's interesting.
So yeah, weed being legal has done wonders for the health of people who smoke weed.
So, um, when I do the video, I'm going to go to sleep now.
I love going to sleep when I'm tired.
Uh, even if I don't go to sleep right away, it's just great to lie down.
And when I get up, I'll finish these stairs.
It'll take a while, a couple more hours, but, but then, yeah, I could program it.
That interesting looking forum building.
And then, um, do the other buildings.
I think that'll pretty much, yeah, wrap up maybe region quickly.
Oh, the other interesting thing I found in region seven was there's this building that said on the map, um, it said Campo de Martis.
Um, the camp, um, the, the field of Mars.
And that was the original field of Mars and, you know, in Rome, but this was an actual converted into the fields, um, of Mars.
Like when it was really built up Rome.
So I said fields, well, fields for what?
Well, if it was modern day, it would be soccer fields.
And so I thought, well, was that Pallon fields?
Cause that was the, the, the sport that they played in the past.
Um, it was like, uh, the best way to describe it would be like soccer.
It was soccer, but you can use your hands.
And, you know, the ball was like a soccer ball.
It was just a little bit smaller and they feel it was, it was, uh, a sack of leather that they filled up with, uh, air and yeah.
And, um, but they would like bump into each other and, you know, but, uh, but, uh, but, oh, but the thing, but they would bounce it off of a 20 meter high wall.
And, uh, but the thing was, is that when, you know, Wikipedia says that their, uh, field, uh, their Pallon fields is, uh, 86 meters by 16 meters.
So I looked at the field that was on the map and lo and behold, it was 172 meters long, which is two Pallon fields end to end.
So, and it was just big enough kind of, but two of them side by side too on each side.
So eight of them.
So you had like, yeah, eight of them.
So that's really cool.
It set up perfectly.
So it was a Pallon field and right next to the Pallon field, um, is just for people playing.
Cause I didn't see room for a stadium, but I should probably draw some in for spectators.
Um, but, um, but right next to that was this thing called what's that?
It was like a conisterium.
So I Googled it and the words was like, yeah, in ancient Rome, the place where they would pay the soldiers and take census and count votes.
And I looked at it and, and so that's what I labeled it.
And so that was pretty cool.
Anyways, we stopped, stopped in for almost 10 minutes.
Talk to you tomorrow.
Maybe we'll be done with region seven and working on the region 16.
All right, uh, later.
